name: HD (Higgs-Demars)
description: The HD (Higgs-Demars) method is an advanced 2x2 speedsolving technique. It involves solving one face first, then using HD-specific algorithms to complete the puzzle. The method leverages a streamlined algorithm set related to CLL, allowing for very low move counts and fast solves.

steps.name: Solve First Face
steps.description: Solve one complete face of the 2x2 cube. Unlike the Ortega method, pieces should be correctly permuted relative to each other on this face, forming a proper layer.
tips: Plan the entire first face during inspection
Try to influence the second layer during the first face solve
Color neutrality greatly improves efficiency for this step

steps.name: HD Algorithms
steps.description: Apply HD algorithms to solve the remaining layer in one step. These algorithms simultaneously orient and permute all remaining pieces, similar to CLL but with HD-specific optimizations.
tips: Learn to recognize cases from multiple angles
Some cases have multiple algorithm options — choose the most ergonomic one

pros: Very low move count (11-15 moves average)
One-look second step for fast finishes
cons: Requires learning a significant number of algorithms
First face must be solved correctly (not just oriented)
Less widely documented than Ortega or EG
Recognition can be difficult at first
creator: Higgs & Demars
